title: JARVIS Step 31 — Read-Only Ask UI Wiring (answers only, no execution)
purpose: Wire the existing display-only 'Talk to JARVIS' conversation shell in templates/jarvis.html to the answer-only POST /api/jarvis/ask endpoint shipped in Step 30. The operator can now ask read-only questions from the page. The UI adds no execution, refresh, broker, paper, live, or mutation controls; it posts only {question} and renders answers or refusals.
relation_to_steps_20_30: Step 26 shipped the display-only conversation shell; Step 28 the pure safety classifier; Step 29 the tests-first ask contract; Step 30 the answer-only POST /api/jarvis/ask handler. Step 31 connects the existing shell to that endpoint with a strictly read-only fetch — no new endpoint, no refresh, no chat logs.
files_changed: templates/jarvis.html — enabled the conversation input (id=jvAskInput), replaced the disabled 'Send disabled' span with a non-button, non-form 'Ask read-only' span (id=jvAskBtn, role=button, tabindex=0), added an aria-live answer area (id=jvAskOut), added the persistent warning 'JARVIS answers only. No execution. No trading control.', changed the panel tag from PLANNING MODE to READ-ONLY, made suggested chips click-to-fill (no auto-submit), and added the wireAsk() JS inside the existing render IIFE that POSTs only {question} to /api/jarvis/ask and renders answer/safety_class/refused/refusal_reason/sources_used.

tests/test_jarvis_route.py — updated three obsolete Step 26 shell guards (text now asserts the persistent warning instead of 'Planning mode only'; the no-working-controls guard drops the two ask-route forbids but keeps button/form/onclick/submit/post/refresh; the input test now asserts the input is enabled and labeled 'Ask read-only'). Added a Step 31 section with UI safety tests and POST integration tests (safe -> answer, forbidden trading -> refused, status shape unchanged).

tests/test_jarvis_ask_contract.py — repointed the obsolete Step 29 template ask-absence guard (renamed test_template_has_no_execution_or_refresh_control); it drops the now-false '/api/jarvis/ask absent in template' check and keeps the button/form/onclick/submit/post/refresh-absence checks.
explicitly_not_done: No new endpoint (uses the existing Step 30 POST /api/jarvis/ask only).
No <button>, <form>, onclick, type=submit, or method=post in the template.
No chat logs, no localStorage/sessionStorage/indexedDB/cookies, no file writes.
No execution/broker/paper/live controls; no trade run or simulation.

ui_wiring.input: id=jvAskInput, enabled, placeholder 'Ask a read-only question…', Enter submits.
ui_wiring.submit_affordance: id=jvAskBtn, a span with role=button + tabindex=0 labeled 'Ask read-only' (NOT a real button/submit element; click + Enter/Space trigger ask()).
ui_wiring.payload: fetch('/api/jarvis/ask', {method:'POST', headers:{Content-Type/Accept json}, body: JSON.stringify({question: q})}) — question is the ONLY field.
ui_wiring.render: answer / safety_class / refused status / refusal_reason (when present) / sources_used (when present); all values escaped via esc().
ui_wiring.chips: five suggested safe questions; clicking a chip fills the input (focus) but never auto-submits.
ui_wiring.persistent_warning: 'JARVIS answers only. No execution. No trading control.' shown in the banner.
ui_wiring.refusals: forbidden questions are rendered as REFUSED with the classifier reason; the UI never attempts to override or execute them.

non_execution_guarantees: Template adds no <button>, <form>, onclick, submit, or method=post control.
No refresh endpoint or wiring introduced.
Only the question field is posted; no command/action/execute field is sent.

updated_step_26_guards_in_test_jarvis_ro: test_jarvis_conversation_shell_text_appears — asserts the persistent warning instead of 'Planning mode only'.
test_jarvis_conversation_shell_has_no_working_controls — drops the two ask-route forbids; keeps button/form/onclick/submit/post/refresh.
test_jarvis_conversation_shell_input_is_disabled -> renamed test_jarvis_conversation_shell_input_is_enabled_read_only_ask — asserts the input is enabled and the 'Ask read-only' span control.
repointed_step_29_guard_in_test_jarvis_a: test_template_has_no_ask_fetch -> renamed test_template_has_no_execution_or_refresh_control — drops the now-false '/api/jarvis/ask absent in template' check; keeps button/form/onclick/submit/post/refresh-absence checks.

open_conflict_awaiting_decision.why_it_fails: It still asserts '/api/jarvis/ask' is absent from templates/jarvis.html. Step 31 intentionally wires the template to that endpoint, so this absence claim is now factually obsolete — identical to the Step 26/28 ask-absence guards that were retired in Step 30.
open_conflict_awaiting_decision.scope_issue: tests/test_jarvis_conversation_safety.py is NOT in Step 31's allowed-files list, so per the Step 30 lesson it was left UNTOUCHED rather than edited unilaterally or worked around by hiding the route string.
open_conflict_awaiting_decision.recommended_resolution: Repoint this guard the same honest way as Step 30: drop the obsolete '/api/jarvis/ask absent' token, keep the still-valid button/form/onclick/submit/post and '/api/jarvis/refresh' absence checks. Awaiting explicit authorization to touch this one out-of-scope test file.

validation.result: 198 passed, 1 failed — the only failure is the obsolete out-of-scope ask-absence guard noted in open_conflict_awaiting_decision.

recommended_next_safe_step: After this UI wiring is committed and green, an optional Step 32 could add a tiny client-side rate guard / input length cap (still read-only) and a live smoke memo confirming the page asks and renders refusals correctly. No execution, refresh, or trading path is ever added.
conclusion: The display-only conversation shell is now wired to the answer-only POST /api/jarvis/ask with a strictly read-only fetch: it posts only {question}, renders answers or refusals, persists nothing, and adds no button/form/refresh/execution/trading control. app.py, jarvis_conversation_safety.py, and the /api/jarvis/status shape are unchanged. One obsolete ask-absence guard in an out-of-scope test file is flagged for the user's decision rather than edited unilaterally.
